business_type: Managed care / vertically integrated health services
red_flag: CMS regulatory pressure + 2026 guidance reset; stock -53% from 2024 high at ~$281
why_interesting: Optum vertical integration creates unmatched cost-advantage flywheel; $281 price is near multi-year low on a business with durable earnings power; guidance reset and regulatory overhang likely priced in
reason_for_action: Best-in-class managed care with genuine vertical moat; -53% from peak and $281 price represents a multi-year entry setup not previously available

red_flag: Management guided 5–13% sales decline in 2026; LLY holds 60% GLP-1 share vs. Novo 40%; China/Brazil/Canada exclusivity lapses
why_interesting: GLP-1 category creator now trades ~75% below 2024 peak; oral Wegovy launched; pipeline and franchise durability warrant full analysis at this price; not yet analyzed
reason_for_action: Stock -75% from peak on acknowledged near-term share loss; but franchise depth and oral GLP-1 optionality justify a full analysis to determine if the discount is temporary or structural

red_flag: DOJ antitrust investigation into debit network practices; fintech disintermediation long-term risk
why_interesting: Duopoly payment rail earning fee on every electronic transaction globally; at ~$314 near 52wk low on 29x PE after earnings beat; cross-border and value-added services compounding
reason_for_action: Highest-quality payment franchise at 52-week low; regulatory overhang creates entry that has not been available for years; not yet analyzed

red_flag: HIV franchise maturity; lenacapavir PrEP label expansion uncertain; oncology pipeline still early
why_interesting: HIV franchise generates exceptional FCF on a recurring basis; oncology pipeline (trodelvy, magrolimab successor) undervalued by market; trading at undemanding valuation for FCF profile
reason_for_action: Undervalued FCF fortress with optionality from oncology; healthcare sector weakness has created entry that didn't exist 18 months ago; not yet analyzed

red_flag: Skyrizi/Rinvoq concentrated revenue; biosimilar Humira erosion not fully complete; aesthetics cyclical
why_interesting: Skyrizi and Rinvoq offsetting Humira loss ahead of consensus expectations; dividend yield growing; healthcare selloff creating entry in a business with durable cash generation
reason_for_action: Faster-than-expected Humira replacement with two blockbusters; combined with sector selloff offering an undemanding entry in a high-FCF compounder not yet analyzed

red_flag: Valuation demands continued volume growth; IAA competition; total-loss frequency cyclical
why_interesting: Irreproducible two-sided auction network protected by land moat; near net-cash balance sheet; 40%+ EBITDA margins; not yet analyzed; quality is exceptional
reason_for_action: Exceptional business quality with genuinely irreproducible moat; not yet analyzed and worth a full 8-umbrella look to establish a baseline thesis

red_flag: Industrial and auto end-market recovery timeline uncertain; Maxim integration still digesting
why_interesting: Best-in-class analog portfolio spanning industrial, healthcare, auto; secular content-per-unit growth; industrial downcycle has compressed valuation creating a setup not seen since 2022
reason_for_action: Industrial downcycle has reset ADI to an attractive entry; quality of portfolio and content-per-unit secular drivers are underappreciated at current price; not yet analyzed

business_type: Contract research / real-world data / health analytics
red_flag: Moderate leverage; pharma R&D cost-cutting compresses CRO demand near-term
why_interesting: Unique combination of CRO scale, real-world data assets, and AI-enabled trial platform; pharma cost-cutting cycle has depressed valuation; not yet analyzed
reason_for_action: Differentiated CRO + data platform with defensible moat; pharma capex downcycle has created a valuation opportunity in a business with durable long-term growth; not yet analyzed

business_type: Enterprise software / cloud platform / AI infrastructure
red_flag: Azure growth deceleration risk; OpenAI cost drag not yet contained; EU antitrust exposure
why_interesting: Best enterprise software franchise with Azure+Copilot monetization at scale; irreplaceable Office/Teams installed base; unmatched FCF and balance sheet
reason_for_action: Highest quality but valuation demands continued near-perfect execution; no compelling price dislocation at present; revisit on any 15%+ drawdown

red_flag: AI Siri still behind; China revenue 20% of total; App Store regulatory exposure
why_interesting: 2B+ installed base; Services at 15%+ growth compounding; switching costs among deepest in consumer tech
reason_for_action: Irreplaceable ecosystem and services flywheel but current price leaves insufficient margin of safety; watch for meaningful dislocation

red_flag: AWS competitive pressure from Azure/GCP; retail margins compressed if macro softens; Alexa AI lag
why_interesting: AWS FCF engine compounding; $50B+ advertising business at near-100% margins; logistics flywheel creating durable retail advantage
reason_for_action: Premium quality at a full price; AWS and ads flywheel are compelling but valuation requires durable 15%+ earnings growth; watch for sector correction entry

business_type: AI accelerator / GPU compute / CUDA platform
red_flag: AI capex consolidation risk if hyperscaler spend plateaus; AMD and custom ASIC competition intensifying; export control restrictions
why_interesting: AI training and inference monopoly; CUDA moat deepest in tech; FCF extraordinary
reason_for_action: Best AI franchise but valuation prices in sustained hyper-growth; a genuine entry requires a meaningful correction; watch for macro-driven AI capex derating

red_flag: AI infrastructure capex ($60B+/yr) diluting near-term FCF; regulatory fragmentation of social platforms; Reality Labs burn
why_interesting: Social graph monopoly monetized at scale; AI ad efficiency driving ARPU; FCF still massive despite capex step-up
reason_for_action: High quality at a reasonable price but social platform regulation and capex commitment create uncertainty that warrants watching for a better entry than current levels

red_flag: AI-native search (Perplexity, ChatGPT search) threatens query volume long-term; DOJ antitrust remedies could force structural change
why_interesting: Search moat intact near-term; GCP accelerating; YouTube undermonetized; massive net cash position; cheapest of mega-cap tech on forward earnings
reason_for_action: Cheapest mega-cap tech with genuine search moat but AI disruption thesis creates a binary risk that makes waiting for a clearer signal prudent

red_flag: DOJ payment network scrutiny; Visa advancing — both cover the duopoly; premium valuation
why_interesting: Cross-border and value-added services growing faster than core; identical business model to Visa with slightly higher growth
reason_for_action: Visa deep_dive covers the payment duopoly; MA will be assessed comparatively; independent deep dive not needed this cycle — monitor alongside V analysis

red_flag: Tirzepatide supply constraints; Alzheimer donanemab reimbursement uncertainty; valuation demands flawless execution
why_interesting: Holds 60% GLP-1 market share; tirzepatide outperforming semaglutide; Alzheimer pipeline could be transformational
reason_for_action: Best GLP-1 franchise but current valuation prices in multi-decade dominance; Novo deep_dive will provide comparative context; monitor for a meaningful correction

business_type: AI custom silicon / networking / enterprise software
red_flag: VMware integration execution; high leverage post-acquisition; concentration in 3 hyperscaler ASIC customers
why_interesting: AI ASIC custom silicon at Google/Meta/Apple scale; VMware cross-sell driving FCF step-up; unique combination of software annuities and AI silicon
reason_for_action: Exceptional quality but integration leverage and customer concentration create uncertainty; wait for VMware integration to prove out before committing a deep dive slot

red_flag: China export restriction revenue headwind in 2026; High NA EUV ramp slower than expected; Dutch government restriction risk
why_interesting: Only company in the world producing EUV lithography; multi-year backlog at €41.9B; no substitute; 2026 revenue guidance €34-39B
reason_for_action: Monopoly moat is irreplaceable but €1,204/share reflects that; China headwind and full valuation mean there's no margin of safety at current price; watch for AI capex pause

red_flag: Taiwan geopolitical risk is existential; US/Japan fab cost overhang; customer concentration (Apple 25%+)
why_interesting: Every leading-edge chip is made here; AI demand driving multi-year fab expansion; CoWoS packaging bottleneck resolved
reason_for_action: Greatest foundry in history with unmatched quality but geopolitical risk is genuinely non-trivial and valuation offers limited margin for it; monitor for event-driven dislocation

red_flag: Medtronic Hugo and J&J Ottava competitive entry; procedure recovery has already repriced the stock to premium
why_interesting: da Vinci installed base creates captive instrument/service annuity; switching costs are genuinely surgical; capital-light instrument stream growing
reason_for_action: Near-perfect business but trades at 60x+ forward earnings; competitive entry from Medtronic and J&J is the first real threat in 20 years; wait for a re-rating opportunity

red_flag: Mark Leonard succession; acquisition pace must sustain to justify valuation; ROIC dilution as deal sizes grow
why_interesting: Unmatched capital allocation discipline; 100+ VMS acquisitions annually; Lumine/Topicus spinoffs extending runway; CSU is the template for serial compounders
reason_for_action: Greatest capital allocator in software but price reflects multi-decade compounding; succession risk and valuation make a current entry too dependent on continuation of perfection

red_flag: Debt issuance cyclicality; Moody's Analytics organic growth moderating; SPGI advancing covers the pair
why_interesting: Oligopoly ratings with guaranteed revenue on every bond issued; MA analytics growing recurring subscription mix
reason_for_action: SPGI deep dive will cover the ratings duopoly adequately; MCO is the same thesis at slightly lower quality; monitor alongside SPGI analysis

red_flag: IHS Markit synergy realization incomplete; debt issuance cyclical; Market Intelligence competitive from Bloomberg
why_interesting: S&P 500 index licensing is a royalty on global passive AUM; ratings on every debt issuance; Market Intelligence fastest-growing segment
reason_for_action: Exceptional franchise but IHS Markit integration at full valuation leaves limited margin; revisit on a debt-market stress event that creates cyclical pressure

red_flag: CFPB/FHFA regulatory risk to mandatory FICO score requirement; VantageScore gaining ground with some lenders
why_interesting: FICO score is mandated in 99% of US mortgage originations; pricing power has been extraordinary; near-monopoly
reason_for_action: Regulatory risk to the mandate is the single biggest tail risk; at current extreme valuation (90x+ earnings) any hint of mandate removal would be devastating; wait for a safer setup

business_type: Index licensing / ESG / real estate analytics
red_flag: Passive AUM reversal would mechanically cut MSCI revenue; high leverage vs. asset-light appearance
why_interesting: AUM fee on $14T+ passive assets; every ETF manager pays MSCI; sticky and growing subscription analytics
reason_for_action: Tax on global passive investing is a wonderful business but already well-priced; a meaningful market correction that shrinks AUM is the natural entry trigger — not today

red_flag: Rate normalization reduces interest rate futures volumes; BrokerTec and crypto exchange competition
why_interesting: Global rates, commodities, and equity derivatives clearing; volume grows with volatility; interest income an added kicker at current rate levels
reason_for_action: High quality exchange with defensive volume characteristics but valuation reflects it; no specific catalyst creating a dislocated entry today

business_type: Energy futures / mortgage technology / data services
red_flag: ICE Mortgage Technology integration slower than expected; energy transition long-term structural risk to Brent futures
why_interesting: Brent crude benchmark creates irreplaceable energy futures; ICE Mortgage Technology cloud platform is multi-year compounder if housing recovers
reason_for_action: Good quality but mortgage tech integration overhang and energy transition risk make it less immediately compelling than CME or CBOE; monitor for housing catalyst

business_type: Options exchange / market data / volatility products
red_flag: SPX options near-monopoly challenged by 24-hour trading expansion; 0DTE structural volume could reverse if regulation changes
why_interesting: SPX options near-monopoly; 0DTE volume structurally growing; market data subscription layer compounding
reason_for_action: Solid franchise but smaller scale than CME; 0DTE volume is a structural tailwind that deserves analysis but not ahead of the 8 deep_dive names selected

red_flag: Single-industry concentration (insurance); ISO data monopoly challenged by insurer self-building
why_interesting: ISO loss costs and data are industry mandated inputs; P&C hardening cycle drives premium growth directly; recurring 95%+ revenues
reason_for_action: Excellent insurance data monopoly but recently divested Financial Services/Energy businesses create a pure-play that is still re-rating; wait for a cleaner valuation setup

red_flag: Bioprocessing recovery timeline uncertain; Veralto spinoff reduces DBS flywheel scale; China biopharma capex soft
why_interesting: DBS operational system drives consistent operating leverage; Cytiva bioprocessing in GLP-1 supply chain; recovering from post-COVID normalization
reason_for_action: Quality compounder but bioprocessing recovery timing is uncertain and current valuation assumes imminent resumption; a cleaner recovery signal would trigger deep_dive

business_type: IT workflow automation / enterprise AI agent platform
red_flag: Valuation requires multi-year perfect execution; AI agent commoditization risk if workflow automation becomes table-stakes
why_interesting: ITSM workflow automation deeply embedded; AI agent TAM expansion is real and NOW is well-positioned; near 52wk low
reason_for_action: Near 52wk low is interesting but valuation still demands high-growth execution at 50x+ earnings; quality is unquestionable but wait for a further reset before committing a deep dive slot

red_flag: Agentforce agent monetization unproven at scale; Microsoft Dynamics and HubSpot competitive pressure; near low
why_interesting: CRM market leader with deep enterprise workflow lock-in; margin inflection from 18% to 30%+ operating margin; Agentforce could be a new TAM expansion
reason_for_action: Good setup near 52wk low with margin inflection story but Agentforce monetization uncertainty warrants waiting for Q2-Q3 2026 evidence before a full analysis commitment

business_type: Tax software / SMB accounting / financial data
red_flag: IRS Direct File free alternative threatening TurboTax; AI bookkeeping tools threatening QuickBooks SMB
why_interesting: TurboTax + QuickBooks installed bases are remarkably sticky; Credit Karma data asset building; Mailchimp integrating
reason_for_action: IRS Direct File threat is real and structurally changes the long-term TurboTax TAM; this risk needs to be assessed before committing a deep dive slot

business_type: Electronic design automation / AI chip design tools
red_flag: EDA duopoly with SNPS; customer concentration in top 10 chip designers; AI chip design complexity may shift to internal tools
why_interesting: Every AI chip is designed on Cadence or Synopsys tools; recurring license model; growing intelligent system design revenue
reason_for_action: Outstanding business but SNPS and CDNS are functionally a duopoly — analyzing one covers both; SNPS has the simulation expansion story; monitor CDNS alongside

business_type: EDA / chip simulation / Ansys system verification
red_flag: Ansys acquisition integration complexity; FTC scrutiny; chip-to-system platform requires execution on multiple fronts
why_interesting: EDA + Ansys simulation creates a chip-to-system verification platform that is unique; every AI chip manufacturer depends on Synopsys tools
reason_for_action: Above-budget: Unique chip-to-system platform worth a full analysis but all 8 deep_dive slots are committed to higher-conviction entry setups; prioritize in next cycle

red_flag: WATCHMAN pricing pressure; EP market competition intensifying from Abbott; near 52wk low
why_interesting: EP and structural heart are secular growth markets; BSX has best-in-class execution; near 52wk low on no fundamental change
reason_for_action: Above-budget: Strong secular setup at a low but healthcare deep_dive slots are allocated to higher-dislocation opportunities (UNH, NOVO, GILD, ABBV, IQV); prioritize in next cycle

red_flag: China export restriction headwind; semi equipment cyclicality; DOJ investigation into technology transfer
why_interesting: Broadest semiconductor equipment portfolio; services recurring revenue growing; AI-driven leading-edge capex durable
reason_for_action: Above-budget: Broad equipment portfolio and AI capex tailwind are compelling but ADI covers the semiconductor setup; analyze AMAT in next cycle alongside KLAC

red_flag: China export restriction exposure; process control commoditization risk at mature nodes
why_interesting: Process control monopoly at leading edge; defect inspection is non-optional; growing services recurring revenue
reason_for_action: Above-budget: Process control monopoly with strong moat but AMAT/KLAC both advance semi equipment; ADI deep_dive covers the semiconductor cycle; prioritize KLA in next cycle

red_flag: Extreme leverage by design (~7x EBITDA); DoD pricing investigations; Boeing/Airbus delivery slowdowns reduce new platform content
why_interesting: Sole-source aftermarket creates captive pricing power; aerospace cycle is secular; FCF exceptional in normal operations
reason_for_action: Exceptional pricing power moat but leverage is intentionally extreme and creates fragility in a recession; CPRT's moat is comparable with far superior balance sheet; prefer CPRT this cycle

red_flag: Pet ownership spending discretionary pressure; Heska/Zoetis competition growing; valuation extreme
why_interesting: Captive reagent annuity on 100K+ installed analyzers; vet practice standard; near net-cash
reason_for_action: Exceptional business but at 50x+ forward earnings with no compelling valuation entry; wait for discretionary pet spending pressure to create a dislocation

business_type: Life sciences CRM / regulatory / clinical cloud software
red_flag: Post-Salesforce migration complete but Vault growth must sustain to justify premium; small pharma spending cuts near-term
why_interesting: CRM and Vault are the industry standard for pharma operations; Salesforce migration risk fully resolved; near net-cash balance sheet
reason_for_action: Outstanding business with complete switching-cost moat but partial report already exists; full analysis warranted when Vault clinical growth rate accelerates; monitor

red_flag: China luxury softness; succession from sixth-generation family; valuation at 50x+ earnings offers no margin of safety
why_interesting: Birkin scarcity moat is genuinely irreproducible; pricing power has no ceiling; near net-cash; best operating margins in luxury
reason_for_action: Perfect business at an imperfect price; 50x earnings requires sustained 15%+ growth from a business already mature in core markets; wait for China luxury recovery to assess entry

company: LVMH Moet Hennessy Louis Vuitton SE
red_flag: China luxury slowdown affecting Fashion & Leather; cognac/wines under cyclical pressure; conglomerate discount vs. pure plays
why_interesting: Unmatched luxury portfolio breadth; aspirational demand structurally resilient; Louis Vuitton alone could justify the market cap
reason_for_action: China luxury cycle softness creates a possible entry but thesis needs deeper work; monitor alongside Hermes; both are above-budget even if valuation were compelling

business_type: Enterprise resource planning / cloud SaaS migration
red_flag: S/4HANA cloud migration customer inertia; Oracle and Workday competing on modules; German government customer concentration
why_interesting: ERP system-of-record for 77% of global transactional revenue; S/4HANA migration multi-year recurring revenue step-up; irreplaceable in large enterprise
reason_for_action: Cloud migration tailwind is real and durable but current valuation prices in most of the upside; monitor for a reset driven by macro or FX that creates a better entry

business_type: Compliance / legal / tax / health information software
red_flag: AI disruption of legal research (LexisNexis competition); digital revenue mix could plateau
why_interesting: Mission-critical compliance software with 60%+ digital revenue; recurring subscription model deeply embedded in law firms and tax authorities
reason_for_action: Quality compounder at full price; European regulatory software is defensible but RELX covers the same segment better; monitor alongside RELX

red_flag: LexisNexis AI competition from Harvey and Westlaw; Elsevier open-access regulatory pressure
why_interesting: LexisNexis + Elsevier AI-enhanced workflows embedded in law and science; sticky subscription model; risk analytics growing
reason_for_action: Above-budget: RELX is a genuine quality compounder at a reasonable price but all deep_dive slots committed; prioritize for first cycle review

business_type: Consumer and commercial credit bureau / identity services
red_flag: D2C Experian Boost margin dilution; EM currency exposure; Equifax and TransUnion compete directly
why_interesting: B2B credit bureau moat plus D2C consumer credit monitoring growing; EM expansion in Brazil and India early innings
reason_for_action: Good quality bureau with EM growth optionality but no specific setup; monitor for EM currency correction creating an entry in the international business

red_flag: China fraud investigation overhang; Enhertu crowded ADC market; management targets ambitious
why_interesting: ADC and rare disease pipeline best-in-class execution; oncology franchise growing 20%+; Farxiga cardiorenal expanding
reason_for_action: Strong execution track record but China investigation adds uncertainty; monitor for resolution — if cleared at a lower price, warrants a full analysis

red_flag: Succession post-Buffett creates unknowable outcome; cash pile ($300B+) drag on returns; conglomerate discount structural
why_interesting: Fortress balance sheet; insurance float compounding; near 52wk low; $300B+ cash creates extraordinary downside protection and option value
reason_for_action: Near 52wk low with enormous cash hoard is interesting but succession risk and the challenge of analyzing a conglomerate make this a watch-and-confirm situation; deep dive when Buffett succession is clearer

red_flag: Housing turnover volume at 30-year lows suppressing project demand; SRS Distribution leverage; rate sensitivity
why_interesting: Pro distribution moat; SRS Distribution acquisition extends to large repair/remodel market; housing recovery is a genuine multi-year catalyst if rates fall
reason_for_action: Near 52wk low with housing recovery setup but thesis depends on rate cuts materializing; SRS leverage adds risk; monitor for rate inflection to create cleaner entry
